goal: Refactor bin/install.js (5,687 lines) from monolith to strict 'one file = one concern' modular architecture. Direct fork of GSD's install.js (6,667 lines); both projects treat this as their biggest maintenance liability. Operator 2026-04-22: 'i dont want a 6k install.js so that will need to be refactored to 1 file = one concern and high reusability and modularity to avoid agents stepping on each others toes.' Modularity discipline: (1) each file has ONE concern and ONE public entry point, (2) files small enough that parallel agents can edit different files simultaneously without merge conflicts (target: <300 lines per module, not <500), (3) cross-module calls via explicit imports, no shared mutable state, no global state mutation, (4) per-runtime install LOGIC in bin/install/runtimes/<runtime>.cjs (one file per runtime: claude, cursor, codex, gemini, opencode, aider, continue, roo, + others), (5) CONVERTERS (tool-name translation, path rewriting, frontmatter mutation) in bin/install/converters/<type>.cjs (one file per converter concern — toolname, paths, frontmatter, etc.), (6) SHARED HELPERS in bin/install/lib/<concern>.cjs (fs-utils.cjs, diff.cjs, tarball.cjs — one file per util concern), (7) DISPATCH ladder in bin/install.js stays but shrinks to <200 lines; only wires modules, doesn't implement, (8) per-runtime tests in tests/install/<runtime>.test.cjs — isolated from other runtimes' changes, (9) per-converter tests in tests/install/converters/<type>.test.cjs. Rule: if a function touches more than one runtime OR more than one converter concern, split it. Parallel-agent safety check: two agents editing install/runtimes/claude.cjs and install/runtimes/cursor.cjs simultaneously should never conflict. Not a prerequisite for 63-28 but strongly recommended BEFORE 63-28 lands manifest-emit logic — adding new modes to the 5,687-line monolith guarantees toe-stepping.
